channel.title: Responsible AI & Neurodivergence, Naba Rizvi
channel.description: Daily curated feed on responsible AI for neurodivergent people and serious mental illness.

item.title: Responsible AI in mental healthcare: policy directions and stakeholder insights, Frontiers in Public Health, 2026
item.description: Synthesizes stakeholder insights into policy directions for governing AI in mental healthcare, emphasizing human oversight, informed consent, privacy, and inclusive design.

item.title: DisaBench: A Participatory Evaluation Framework for Disability Harms in Language Models, arXiv, 2026
item.description: Proposes a participatory benchmark, co-designed with disabled people, to surface disability-related harms in language models that conventional top down metrics overlook.

item.title: VERA-MH: Validation of Ethical and Responsible AI in Mental Health, arXiv, 2026
item.description: Introduces a validation framework for judging whether AI mental health tools behave ethically and safely, aiming to standardize how responsible-AI claims in this space are tested.

item.title: Rethinking AI Psychosis: Misnomers, Conceptual Limits, and Existential Drift, arXiv, 2026
item.description: Argues the popular label AI psychosis is a misnomer that flattens distinct experiences, and proposes more careful concepts for describing how sustained chatbot use can unsettle a user's sense of reality.

item.title: AI Psychosis: Does Conversational AI Amplify Delusion-Related Language?, arXiv, 2026
item.description: Empirically tests whether conversational AI amplifies delusion-related language, probing the mechanism by which extended chatbot exchanges might reinforce rather than challenge distorted beliefs.
